(r 3') [...] juniper, thyme, plant of [...]. You fill [...] resin of baluhhu-plant and ... [...]. You cook [...] in beer in a tangussu-vessel (and) you cool (it) down. You pour [...] of a bat into her womb and ... [...].

(r 7') You rub together [...] ... (and) imhur-līm-plant (and then) she drinks it in beer on an empty stomach [...].

r 8'8'

[...] u₂EME.UR.GI₇ 1niš ta-mar-raq ina KAŠ x [...]

(r 8') you rub together [...] (and) hound's-tongue (and then) [...] in beer ... [...].

[... u₂]⸢IGI-lim u₂IGI.20⸣ [u₂]⸢ḪAR⸣.ḪAR u₂KUR.KUR KUŠ₈.⸢LAGAB⸣ [...]

(r 9') [...] imhur-līm-plant, imhur-ešrā-plant, thyme, white hellebore, colocynth [...] ... quickly [...].

r 11'11'

(r 11') You soak [...] ... [...]. You set out flour [...]. [...] into her womb [...].

r 12'12'

[...] ša GIG ša₂ la₃ im-mar ša₂ GIG ša₂ ⸢im⸣-mar u₂IGI.20 ⸢SUḪUŠ⸣ [...]

(r 12') [...] concerning an illness that (the āšipu) does not see (or) concerning an illness that he sees, you will rub together imhur-ešrā-plant, root of [...], (and) ṣaṣumtu-plant (and then) [...] in oil, beer and vinegar.

r 14'14'

* MUNUS UŠ₂-ša₂ KU₅-⸢ma⸣ UŠ₂-⸢ša₂⸣ la₃ [...]

(r 14') "If a woman's blood is blocked or her blood not [...]". Tablet 41 of "If a person's skull suffers from fever". Treatment of ... [...].

r 16'16'

GABA.RI ⸢UNUGki GIM?⸣ [SUMUN]-šu₂ md60—ik-ṣur A-šu₂ ša₂ m[dUTU—MU ...]

(r 16') Copy of Uruk, according to its [original]. Anu-ikṣur, son of Šamaš-iddin [...] wrote and checked (it). Tablet of Šamaš-iddin ... [...]. The one who fears Anu [and] Ištar will not take (it) away.
